name: Steve Chabot
subject: Helping get us through
body:
I want you to know I am working tirelessly, and across party lines, to help Ohio get through the challenging times created by the COVID-19 crisis.
No images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-jhhuklil-kujkulhluu-k/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhhuklil-kujkulhluu-r/) Dear friend, I want you to know I am working tirelessly, and across party lines, to help Ohio get through the challenging times created by the COVID-19 crisis.I've helped secure support and relief for hard-hit workers, families, small businesses, and health care workers, led the charge to modernize the nation's pandemic response systems and joined a bipartisan push to mobilize American industry to produce supplies needed on the front lines. As the Ranking Member of the House Small Business Committee, I am especially fighting to help Ohio small businesses and their workers during these challenging times, including helping craft and pass the [Paycheck Protection Program](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhhuklil-kujkulhluu-y/) that begins processing applications today to help small businesses continue to make payroll.I've also been working with Governor Mike DeWine and medical experts to keep Ohio families informed and help keep our communities safe.By continuing to put aside political differences and work together, Ohio will emerge stronger on the other side of this crisis.If you have a moment, please watch my new [web video](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhhuklil-kujkulhluu-j/) highlighting some of my work to help our communities through this: [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhhuklil-kujkulhluu-t/) Friend, you don't have to be told times are tough, but if you are able, I could use your help to continue spreading our message. **[Please consider donating $5, $10, $25, or more now](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhhuklil-kujkulhluu-i/), **so I can keep sharing my work for our communities with voters.** ** All the best to you and your family,Steve Chabot | [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhhuklil-kujkulhluu-d/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhhuklil-kujkulhluu-h/)

name: Tony Gonzales
subject: We've got to help out!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tony Gonzales for Congress**
body:
|
---
| | |
---
---
| |
---
---
| | | Dear Friend, We are all watching developments in the coronavirus pandemic closely. _This is a time for vigilance, prudence, and wherever possible, generosity._ In this spirit I want to ask all of my supporters to find one meaningful way to help their fellow neighbors during this crisis. Here are some options:
_**Donate to a Food Bank**_ – You can find the nearest one to you [here](https://tonygonzales.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhuajk-olulttitiy-i/). _**Volunteer to Deliver Meals**_ – Feeding America is a wonderful organization that provides meals to needy individuals and families. Learn more [here](https://tonygonzales.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhuajk-olulttitiy-d/). _**Give Blood**_ – With less donors giving blood, hospitals are in desperate need of ALL blood types. The Red Cross has implemented safe ways to give blood during the pandemic. Learn more [here](https://tonygonzales.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhuajk-olulttitiy-h/). If you are a healthy and eligible blood donor, [click here](https://tonygonzales.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhuajk-olulttitiy-k/) to find a blood bank in your community. _**Support Those on the Frontlines**_ – Call ahead to your local first responder stations/clinics/hospitals and ask them about donating food or coffee. Coordinate with them about best drop-off locations and times. _**Become a Senior Sidekick**_ – Even the smallest acts of kindness can make a big difference for the most vulnerable in your neighborhood. Make a phone call to check in, offer to do a grocery run, help with yardwork, the list goes on. Remind them to check the supply of critical items and needed medications—it can take much longer to refill and restock these items than they are used to. _**Support Small Businesses **_– Purchase gift cards or certificates now that can be used at a later time. Our local businesses need our help today. Support local restaurants by purchasing take-out meals and delivering them to seniors or others in need/clinics/first responders, etc. (Always call first.) Some restaurants are now offering to sell their food in bulk thanks to a recent executive order from Governor Abbott. Consider purchasing that food and then donating it to a local food bank - a win-win!
**Together, we will get through this national crisis.** Sincerely, Tony Gonzales
---
---

name: Team Manny
subject: Some GREAT non-profits across our state!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Manny for Senate, All rights reserved
body:
[](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhulikt-uunkhiuw-y/)
---
| |
---|---|---
| Friends, This pandemic has shown me the resilience of our citizens. I'm so proud to call myself a Tennessean and have seen incredible work being done all around to fight this virus. But we still have work to do. Many folks are still sick, unemployed, or need our help! **Would you consider donating to these non-profits doing awesome work in our state? ** Feeding America has launched the COVID-19 Response Fund, a national food- and fundraising effort to support people facing hunger and the food banks who help them. They are working with government leaders to ensure the emergency response includes strong support and flexibility for federal nutrition programs including the School Lunch Program. [FEEDING AMERICA](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhulikt-uunkhiuw-j/) Second Harvest Food Bank of East Tennessee is one of many Second Harvest food banks working to supply food to locally. As more Tennesseans file for unemployment, more people are likely to go hungry. [SECOND HARVEST FOOD BANK OF EAST TENNESSEE](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhulikt-uunkhiuw-t/) The Community Foundation, in partnership with the City of Memphis and several other local leadership institutions, launched the Mid-South COVID-19 Regional Response Fund to provide immediate resources for triage and recovery resources to ensure community resilience. [COMMUNITY FOUNDATION OF MEMPHIS](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhulikt-uunkhiuw-i/) The Tennessee Region of the American Red Cross serves all 95 counties in Tennessee. Right now in this pandemic many folks are going to need donated blood. My wife Maya and I have donated and I encourage you to do so as well. [RED CROSS](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhulikt-uunkhiuw-d/) Northside Neighborhood House promotes the independence of residents in Chattanooga by providing a hand up through education and assistance. They help folks with paying bills, providing education for children, and even operate a thrift shop. [NORTHSIDE NEIGHBORHOOD HOUSE](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhulikt-uunkhiuw-h/) I would greatly appreciate anything you can pitch infor any of these fine organizations or others you may know. This is a difficult time for everyone, but we will come together to beat this! Please continue to pray for our state and our country as we go through this tumultuous time. All the best, Dr. Manny P.S. I hope you'll tune in to my [Virtual Town Hall on Facebook ](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhulikt-uunkhiuw-k/)at 9:30 a.m. central on Saturday to hear exactly why we must send a conservative outsider to represent us in Washington. Copyright © 2019 Paid for by Dr. Manny for Senate, All rights reserved.

PO Box 58068, Nashville, TN 37205 |

name: Nina Ahmad
subject: What is an Auditor General, Anyway?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nina for PA
body:
| |
---
So, what is an Auditor General, anyway? I get that question a lot. With an open seat for Pennsylvania Auditor General, now before we vote on Tuesday, June 2 it is incredibly important to answer. The Auditor General can impact real progressive change in Pennsylvania, but only if we elect the right candidate. Our Auditor General should be an outspoken advocate for the values we share. She is the chief watchdog of taxpayer spending, with a laser focus on how our public investments and dollars can be used wisely and make a difference. As a medical scientist, advocate, and policymaker, that is my vision for this office. As a proven fighter and advocate for progressive values, I will build on the success of the current Auditor General Democrat Eugene DePasquale to make that vision a reality. [If you believe we need an Auditor General who will fight for our progressive values, click here to support my campaign. ](https://act.myngp.com/el/tvIb5cjhvSlEVkesLiGzizkkMpxHJ1LYXoiyMoAJzlk=/i7wdlJoUX_9esgesvZET_nvsVY7h2W4pzEsf7ReEzQk=) The Auditor General oversees over $78 billion in taxpayer money. Oversight isn't enough. As Auditor General, I will make sure that the way we spend our money reflects our shared values. Budgets are living documents that reflect moral choices. With that in mind, I support the growing calls for COVID CARE FOR ALL, paid for in part by rooting out windfalls from mega-corporations making excess profits during the crisis. This is both a fair and clear response which bends the rising public health and overall cost curve of the pandemic. As a respected scientist in the medical field, I understand the complexities of healthcare policy. I know that public investment in healthcare must be guided by data and facts, not the influence of special interests. As our fiscal watchdog, I will make sure politicians in Harrisburg don't manipulate this crisis to simply help their corporate contributors, further restrict healthcare, women's rights, or the choices all working families should be able to make regarding affordable medicine and treatment. [If you believe we need an Auditor General who will make decisions based on facts and evidence and not the influence of special interests, click here to support my campaign.](https://act.myngp.com/el/tvIb5cjhvSlEVkesLiGzizkkMpxHJ1LYXoiyMoAJzlk=/i7wdlJoUX_9esgesvZET_nvsVY7h2W4pzEsf7ReEzQk=) I have always fought for the things I believe, and I will be an advocate for all of us. In Philadelphia, as a Deputy Mayor, I helped to guide over $4 billion in public spending. I launched the city's first Office for Women, directed prevention and job resources to the formerly incarcerated, and worked extensively with the LGBTQ community to end discrimination and promote equality for everyone. As President of NOW, I led efforts to crack down on secret settlements for sexual harassment and non-disclosure cases in government. So what is an Auditor General? If we elect the right one, she's an advocate for all of us and a shield against special interests. That is my pledge to you. [Please empower our campaign today with a contribution so you have a watchdog on your side. clean_body: | | So, what is an Auditor General, anyway? I get that question a lot. With an open seat for Pennsylvania Auditor General, now before we vote on Tuesday, June 2 it is incredibly important to answer. The Auditor General can impact real progressive change in Pennsylvania, but only if we elect the right candidate. Our Auditor General should be an outspoken advocate for the values we share. She is the chief watchdog of taxpayer spending, with a laser focus on how our public investments and dollars can be used wisely and make a difference. As a medical scientist, advocate, and policymaker, that is my vision for this office. As a proven fighter and advocate for progressive values, I will build on the success of the current Auditor General Democrat Eugene DePasquale to make that vision a reality. = The Auditor General oversees over $78 billion in taxpayer money. Oversight isn't enough. As Auditor General, I will make sure that the way we spend our money reflects our shared values. Budgets are living documents that reflect moral choices. With that in mind, I support the growing calls for COVID CARE FOR ALL, paid for in part by rooting out windfalls from mega-corporations making excess profits during the crisis. This is both a fair and clear response which bends the rising public health and overall cost curve of the pandemic. As a respected scientist in the medical field, I understand the complexities of healthcare policy. I know that public investment in healthcare must be guided by data and facts, not the influence of special interests. As our fiscal watchdog, I will make sure politicians in Harrisburg don't manipulate this crisis to simply help their corporate contributors, further restrict healthcare, women's rights, or the choices all working families should be able to make regarding affordable medicine and treatment. = I have always fought for the things I believe, and I will be an advocate for all of us. In Philadelphia, as a Deputy Mayor, I helped to guide over $4 billion in public spending. I launched the city's first Office for Women, directed prevention and job resources to the formerly incarcerated, and worked extensively with the LGBTQ community to end discrimination and promote equality for everyone. As President of NOW, I led efforts to crack down on secret settlements for sexual harassment and non-disclosure cases in government. So what is an Auditor General? If we elect the right one, she's an advocate for all of us and a shield against special interests. That is my pledge to you. = Thank you for your support, Learn more by visiting our website: = = Follow us on Social Media: = = Copyright (C) Nina for PA. All rights reserved. name: Warnock for Georgia
subject: re: your response
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warnock for Georgia
body:
Friend: We're going over survey responses with Reverend Warnock soon - but we noticed we're still missing yours.
---
At this moment in history, it's never been more important to elect Democrat leaders who will fight for the most vulnerable among us. That's why we need to elect Reverend Warnock, restore moral leadership to our government and retake Senate control - but we can't do it without you.
We mean it when we say that your voice is critical to shaping our strategy in this must-win race.
**Please, this is important: Can Reverend Warnock count on you to take just 60 seconds now to share your thoughts? We need to hear from another 24 people by midnight.**
Here's the best link: **[https://surveys.signforgood.com/warnock-april-strategy](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://surveys.signforgood.com/warnock-april-strategy?firstName=&lastName=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=30004&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk146-su-nd")**
Thank you for taking the time - your feedback really makes a difference.
Warnock for Georgia
\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Reverend Warnock Date: Fri, April 03, 2020
Subject: I need your response, friend

| | |
---
Friend,

_We are in a fight for the soul of our nation 's democracy._
As the senior pastor at Ebenezer Baptist Church, the spiritual home of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., I think about Dr. King's words often - especially his urgent call for a "revolution of values." And lately, with politicians like Donald Trump and Mitch McConnell in office, these words ring truer than ever.
Corrupt leaders have silenced millions with systematic voter suppression. They've attacked our health care. They've abused their power, and they've led an all-out assault on the character of our country.
If we don't respond in this moment of crisis with the moral leadership that these issues deserve, what will our country become?
**Friend, that 's why I'm running for U.S. Senate - to restore moral leadership to our government and make sure every single person has a voice in our democracy. ****But I won 't be able to flip Georgia blue without hearing what's important to people like you.** [**So I need your help: Will you please take just a few moments to take our April strategy survey? Your feedback is essential to making sure we flip this seat and take back the Senate for Democrats.**](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://surveys.signforgood.com/warnock-april-strategy?firstName=&lastName=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=30004&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk146-su-nd")
I've dedicated myself to my community and worked hard to help others realize their fullest potential - because I know what it's like to not be born with a seat at the table. We have to make the system work for everyone, and it's exactly what I plan to do in the Senate.
I'm ready to make health care affordable and accessible for all - because one bad diagnosis shouldn't send our families spiraling. Ready to fight for the dignity of workers. Ready to get out the vote, not suppress the vote. Ready to stand up for the kid growing up in the projects and communities across Georgia, who, like me, wants to achieve _their_ American dream.
**I 'm ready to flip the great state of Georgia blue and take back the Senate for Democrats - but I can't do it without you.**
**[Friend, I need your input as soon as possible. Please take our brief survey to help inform our strategy for flipping this seat and taking back the Senate for Democrats.](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://surveys.signforgood.com/warnock-april-strategy?firstName=&lastName=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=30004&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk146-su-nd")**
| | [Take the Survey](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://surveys.signforgood.com/warnock-april-strategy?firstName=&lastName=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=30004&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk146-su-nd")
---
Thanks for sharing your feedback. I'm looking forward to reading your responses.

Reverend Warnock

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: Asking for your input
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado
body:
|
#
Hi folks -- I hope you and your loved ones are staying healthy and safe during this uncertain time. It’s hard to believe how much our lives have changed in just a few short weeks. But, as always, this incredible grassroots team has never ceased to amaze me. **I have some good news: my team just informed me that we officially reached our goal for the first quarter of this election year. That’s huge.** So many of you stepped up to chip in what you could to make a meaningful difference, and I’m so grateful to have your support. Thank you. This week, I’ll be sitting down with my team (virtually!) to go over our priorities and strategy for the next couple months. Before we do, it’s important to me that I get your feedback. While my opponent is busy being Donald Trump’s favorite yes-man, my biggest priority is making sure this campaign is about _you_ and fighting for _your_ interests in Washington. **I wish I could sit down and grab coffee (or a cold beer) with each and every one of you to hear about your concerns. But the next-best way for me to know what’s on your mind is through my quick priorities survey.** [**Will you take a few minutes (two, tops!) to let me know what issues matter most to you in this race?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16885201/179783329/-2089347962?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZDFiMTJmZS1hMjc2LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7JYGMJxDLL30DFdWLO3dKpXfFCP82GZw67zbnDOYoIo=&emci=cc1b12fe-a276-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=cd1b12fe-a276-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=7280715) | [TAKE OUR SURVEY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16885202/179783330/-2089347962?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZDFiMTJmZS1hMjc2LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7JYGMJxDLL30DFdWLO3dKpXfFCP82GZw67zbnDOYoIo=&emci=cc1b12fe-a276-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=cd1b12fe-a276-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=7280715)
---
Now more than ever, we must come together to build a winning movement to give Coloradans a leader they can trust to do what’s right. But the only way we’ll win is if we set our priorities and follow through on them together.
Your feedback is extremely important to me, so I really hope you’ll take some time to fill out my survey today.
Thanks. I’ll be in touch soon.
— John
Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado

clean_body: | Hi folks -- I hope you and your loved ones are staying healthy and safe during this uncertain time. It’s hard to believe how much our lives have changed in just a few short weeks. But, as always, this incredible grassroots team has never ceased to amaze me. I have some good news: my team just informed me that we officially reached our goal for the first quarter of this election year. That’s huge. So many of you stepped up to chip in what you could to make a meaningful difference, and I’m so grateful to have your support. Thank you. This week, I’ll be sitting down with my team (virtually!) to go over our priorities and strategy for the next couple months. Before we do, it’s important to me that I get your feedback. While my opponent is busy being Donald Trump’s favorite yes-man, my biggest priority is making sure this campaign is about you and fighting for your interests in Washington. I wish I could sit down and grab coffee (or a cold beer) with each and every one of you to hear about your concerns. But the next-best way for me to know what’s on your mind is through my quick priorities survey. | Now more than ever, we must come together to build a winning movement to give Coloradans a leader they can trust to do what’s right. But the only way we’ll win is if we set our priorities and follow through on them together. Your feedback is extremely important to me, so I really hope you’ll take some time to fill out my survey today. Thanks. I’ll be in touch soon. — John Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado HICKENLOOPER FOR COLORADO PO Box 18886 Denver, CO 80218 United States

name: Adem Bunkeddeko
subject: Our public housing crisis is a public health crisis
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/r82koxASHnn-NN9AmxvTrfY7CJMyeE-ThkRU0Tk_Dqk=/WVMXw_0wCyRx_0KDogXXsP--nWgvxh0p-U3xAIPiy18=)

Peter,

Since the very start of this campaign, I've been running on the simple truth that our public housing system is broken. Residents in 326 NYCHA developments across NYC -- over 20 of which are located in our district -- are living in unsafe conditions where infestations, heat and hot water outages, and sewage leaks are the norm.

Now, our city is the epicenter of a global pandemic. If there was ever any doubt that our public housing crisis is also a public health crisis, the evidence is the daily struggle of far too many people in our community. Times like these highlight the need for urgent, sweeping reforms to public housing, and I'm the only candidate with a plan that will deliver. **[Will you donate $100, $50, $25, or any amount today to help bring public housing residents the outspoken representation they deserve?](https://act.myngp.com/el/r82koxASHnn-NN9AmxvTrfY7CJMyeE-ThkRU0Tk_Dqk=/o_c8DOCRHzQMOQ7NMh20qcraiAnsZdh26MZTAmuLGa4=)**

******[Express Donate: $25](https://act.myngp.com/el/r82koxASHnn-NN9AmxvTrfY7CJMyeE-ThkRU0Tk_Dqk=/o_c8DOCRHzQMOQ7NMh20qcraiAnsZdh26MZTAmuLGa4=?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=express_lane_25)******
******[Or, donate another amount](https://act.myngp.com/el/r82koxASHnn-NN9AmxvTrfY7CJMyeE-ThkRU0Tk_Dqk=/o_c8DOCRHzQMOQ7NMh20qcraiAnsZdh26MZTAmuLGa4=?express_lane_other=true&refcode=express_lane_other)******

What happens when the safest place to be is at home, but your home is what's making you sick? When one of the most effective ways to avoid exposure to COVID-19 is washing your hands thoroughly and often, but your hot water has been shut off? When those with compromised immune systems are at an even higher risk of contracting the disease, but prolonged exposure to lead paint, mold, grime, and dust resulted in your chronic illness? When it's imperative to avoid close contact with others, but overcrowded housing conditions have made that impossible? This is the reality NYCHA residents face -- and if our leaders aren't going to answer to and act on behalf of our public housing residents, then we will.

******[SUPPORT MY FIGHT FOR OUR PUBLIC HOUSING](https://act.myngp.com/el/r82koxASHnn-NN9AmxvTrfY7CJMyeE-ThkRU0Tk_Dqk=/o_c8DOCRHzQMOQ7NMh20qcraiAnsZdh26MZTAmuLGa4=)******

We know what needs to be done: We need to fully fund NYCHA, bring every development into compliance with federal housing quality standards, and repeal the Faircloth Amendment so we can fund the construction of new public housing units, because the health and safety of our neighbors depend on it. **[Are you with me?](https://act.myngp.com/el/r82koxASHnn-NN9AmxvTrfY7CJMyeE-ThkRU0Tk_Dqk=/o_c8DOCRHzQMOQ7NMh20qcraiAnsZdh26MZTAmuLGa4=)**

Best,
Adem

subject: Five Stories President Trump Doesn’t Want You to Miss
body:
Trump Administration Moving Swiftly to Address Ventilator Shortages | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0b406ed979&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **_FIVE STORIES PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | [**Trump Administration Moving Swiftly to Address Ventilator Shortages**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1c4f6f2c4f&e=3ee4985eea)
_-USA Today_
“In a page taken out of the World War II playbook, the Ford Motor Co., with General Electric, is racing to produce an additional 50,000 new ventilators in 100 days at a converted auto plant in Michigan,” writes Peter Navarro, Director of the White House Office of Trade and Manufacturing Policy.

**🎬****Updated guidelines:** [30 days to slow the spread!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=593d9c7483&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [**A Marshall Plan for Main Street**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9bf1ac0ac5&e=3ee4985eea)
-_Washington Examiner_
“Lower taxes and fewer regulations unleashed the best economy in half a century and gave small businesses the means to hire more employees, pay higher wages, and reinvest capital. These conditions still exist but are being seriously challenged by the coronavirus pandemic,” House Minority Leader Kevin McCarthy writes. The Paycheck Protection Program, already up and running, will help small businesses weather this storm.

**Ivanka Trump:** [Bold action to “preserve our nation’s small businesses”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=48a402e823&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [**Team USA: 50 Companies Join Trump's War on Coronavirus**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8c77fd9927&e=3ee4985eea)
_-Washington Examiner_
“Some 50 major American firms have joined the national war on the coronavirus, many volunteering to shift their focus and even assembly lines to deliver needed supplies to doctors, hospitals, first responders and others on the front lines,” Paul Bedard reports. “Most said that they answered President Trump’s call for help.”
---
|
---
| [**USNS Comfort Arrives in New York City**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c338fd9858&e=3ee4985eea)
-_Politico_
“The USNS Comfort arrived in New York on Monday, bringing a massive Navy hospital ship to help relieve city hospitals overwhelmed by coronavirus patients,” Erin Durkin writes. The ship “was undergoing maintenance when President Donald Trump pledged to deploy it to New York, which was expected to take two weeks but was sped up to eight days.”

🎬 **Watch:** [Hope arrives to the ports of LA and NYC](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=40aa51364e&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [**Pols Face a Coronavirus Test**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=42a744fb55&e=3ee4985eea)
_-The Wall Street Journal_
“Crises have a way of separating the leaderlike wheat from the opportunistic chaff,” Kimberly Strassel writes. “Example: The Trump administration spent this week distributing ventilators, standing up small-business loans, dispatching hospital ships, erecting alternate care facilities . . . Speaker Nancy Pelosi spent this week setting up a new House committee to investigate Donald Trump.”
---

| A Message from Matt!
---
As New Hampshire continues to fight against our invisible enemy I can't help but be overwhelmed by the acts of charity and selflessness that I've seen across the state. Neighbors helping neighbors, communities coming together, people putting their lives and businesses at risk to help others.
I have been proud to join my fellow Granite Staters in their efforts by helping to deliver hand sanitizer from the Flag Hill Distillery to our local first responders and organizing "Thank you medical workers" sign waves in front of our hospitals. I was also very proud this past week to announce our Power of Prayer event, a way to unite our community in prayer safely from their own homes. I hope that everyone is staying safe and healthy and thank you for your support!
\- Matt PS: I want to make sure each and every one of you is thanked for your help and support. We met our goals for the 1st Quarter!!! We faced a mountain and YOU helped us scale it. More details coming later but I just want you to know how much I, personally, appreciate each and every one of you. PSS: You are going to be happy with our Q1 numbers! THANK YOU.

Hoosiers from all over our state are coming together during this difficult time. | | |
---|---|---
Friend,

**We’re #INThisTogether — it’s just that simple. **

Hoosiers from all over our state are coming together like never before during this difficult time. Every single day, we see more incredible stories about Hoosiers making sacrifices to help their neighbors get through the pandemic.

**As Governor Holcomb said, “As we see the surge coming, we see it matched only by a true surge of Hoosier generosity and kindness and determination.” **

We reached out to you last week to share some of our favorite stories, and the response to our message was so overwhelmingly positive that we just had to share some more.

clean_body: Hoosiers from all over our state are coming together during this difficult time. | | | ---|---|--- Friend, We’re #INThisTogether — it’s just that simple. Hoosiers from all over our state are coming together like never before during this difficult time. Every single day, we see more incredible stories about Hoosiers making sacrifices to help their neighbors get through the pandemic. As Governor Holcomb said, “As we see the surge coming, we see it matched only by a true surge of Hoosier generosity and kindness and determination.” We reached out to you last week to share some of our favorite stories, and the response to our message was so overwhelmingly positive that we just had to share some more. We hope you enjoyed these amazing Hoosier stories! We will continue posting all the good stories that come in on our social media accounts— you can find them by searching #INThisTogether on Twitter, or by following Eric on or If you hear a story about Hoosiers lending their neighbors a hand during this time, please share it with us by tweeting the story with #INThisTogether, and you might just get a retweet from us! name: Charles Booker

body:
Can you share with me what’s most important to you and your family? I want to know your worries and make sure our campaign understands what are the most urgent issues people face in the time of COVID-19.
I’m worried.
I’ll be honest with you — this crisis is unlike anything we’ve ever experienced in American history.
COVID-19 has changed the way we live and will continue to impact us for an uncertain amount of time. It has especially hurt working-class families, unprotected workers, brown and black people, and those who have already been neglected by our country’s broken safety net.
My heart goes out to anyone who’s unemployed, putting their life on the line at work, struggling to make ends meet, or scared of what the future holds.
**The only way we’re going to get through this is together.** Especially now in light of this crisis, we are determined to make sure that our leaders represent the people’s interests and support those who are hurting the most in this crisis.
**[Can you share with me what’s most important to you and your family? President Trump, Vice President Pence, and the COVID-19 taskforce have been hard at work to slow the spread and keep Americans safe from the COVID-19.

The president recently extended his 15-day plan for another 30 days to help mitigate infection rates and flatten the curve.

We know that this slowdown has hurt a lot of businesses and workers, and **we’re doing our part to support local restaurants and businesses by sending gift cards and encouraging take out orders, you can learn more about that here:[https://claudiaforcongress.com/support-local-businesses/](http://links.claudiaforcongress.com/u/click?_t=e8d915629893476cb837806f005d02de&_m=f313497bf41a4cbc97f5d8adce6ba0ad&_e=Yi-fvDjxbNF4Brba2gUb7gpAwCssgosfod1-KSH_exh_O0sci_fpGIE_ACWX3hCsagpC8iyWypa9jUd0dw4YX8mRrSBoNpaKHk6UBUFrCZ9jHiYBkf7qZOlYkU3-1bYHKIFO5wa5rl_GXSckIKcWVZMO2lfMKLyq0SA3f33VKykxsPPjRdPpVoqp4QU5o_xbYcj3tEJL3mOcU-C7r7mYQYFJn21ajm5DymPcUpK-B1aOUTd3XkBrcaK4vfDnVWzUZQoNuE41ZCofM9A_1fI4F5dWErrh6MrE3ZgzH5ksCs7a7g_xMdy9cINXf6AzhraRTtbVRptFy3Rh5jLHmgTmhm_lD-1xPYrLOk0Bfh73YmyIEHN8-tF5mm_S1bf2M9ICoVv3ffhuMC9PSwXiiczU4nx4oSkzyl2300WiCXeFZT-As9y0f9OrlKh6ZwTru6RBkZ2gyq1wsFT8rjWKNa2K2wxNpmScy3gA_RRXZt3dvIg%3D)**

As this virus continues to make its way through our country, it's important to stay strong and stay up to date on all the latest health measures. The advice to the public, for the most part, remains the same.
* **SOCIAL DISTANCING** is still recommended.
* **STAY AT HOME** unless you are getting food, picking up medicine, or exercising.
* **IF YOU DEVELOP SYMPTOMS** call your doctor.
* **STAY INFORMED** by visiting this link: [**https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/**](http://links.claudiaforcongress.com/u/click?_t=e8d915629893476cb837806f005d02de&_m=f313497bf41a4cbc97f5d8adce6ba0ad&_e=Yi-fvDjxbNF4Brba2gUb7j8PLGAyfjEC-iPSxit-y5U-iZhk7_mInzkd8opvuwP635lXkfWApO26nMIPJU11lKTDo8Kj9Fp4bqRsGPTAcVsWe0K4Kv9WejUQwYx0eRPZNVouixjtmEh7Ua-UbJdyYWtBxCCWn5ypf4hW54Ch9PV0XQsR0NNRc98YEzNEZS3_LxEY7hsMbXG_XwC9zCLD8MenFye5D0AkTfEgbxtZr31rpIRZp9wkkhwKciWUQUDTJN7hOayEIchsqW6HGPmlhS1vXgGbMqyJp1vFNPdtjYvRhkCYLcmI3c4zPzWO5yfDHEZZDnm4kk3D3NQAfaiZrwH9pUre3NKIfPrhH9F5vUmbY04MwvJuVWGt67sjEc-oZt03l03To1MIoD-TyzYFSqyl0633XzWhbwsqbw0h6qjoqlpH-0g36kAAGuIwoErBQ_YOm977SFoaWc7g3xsFWA%3D%3D)

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Add your name: Trump needs to reopen HealthCare.gov and let people sign up for coverage. Now.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren Democrats
body:
Add your name if you agree: Trump needs to reopen HealthCare.gov during this coronavirus crisis and allow millions of people to get access to health care.... | | | [](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285257ae/45c30650/1f4c49bc2/66e97999/1533021746/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
The coronavirus crisis is exposing so many of the ways our country doesn't work for working people -- and one of the biggest is health care.

Right now, millions of people are losing their jobs, having their hours cut, and staring into economic uncertainty.

But Donald Trump and Mike Pence have made it clear -- even during this pandemic, Americans without health insurance are on their own. They refuse to reopen HealthCare.gov to let people sign up for coverage and get financial assistance to help pay for it.

States like Massachusetts have opened special enrollment periods for uninsured Americans to sign up for health care. But that won't help everyone who needs help across the country.

**This is just another example of why we need Medicare For All. It shouldn't matter whether or not someone has a job, or whether or not there's a pandemic -- health care is a basic human right, and we fight for basic human rights.**

**[But right now, the Trump administration needs to stop playing politics and start saving lives. Add your name if you agree: Trump needs to reopen HealthCare.gov during this coronavirus crisis and allow millions of people to get access to health care.](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285257ae/45c30650/1f4c49bc2/66e97998/1533021746/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTVEwNVBfU0dOX000QV9FXzIwMjAwNDA0X1YxUzFfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-warrendemocrats-reopen-healthcare-gov?source=EMQ05P_SGN_M4A_E_20200404_V1S1_P&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

At a recent press briefing, Mike Pence got asked a simple question: What can people do now to get health insurance if they don't have it?

He put on his best calm, "nothing to see here" voice -- and completely dodged the question. He told reporters not to worry -- health insurance companies have agreed to waive some people's copays.

But what about the millions of Americans who have no insurance or are losing their insurance because they're losing their jobs? What kind of help are they going to get to cover their health care costs in the middle of a health care crisis?

Now, here's some good news: A bunch of states that run their own health insurance marketplaces are letting people sign up right now. These states are **California, Colorado, Connecticut, the District of Columbia, Maryland, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Nevada, New York, Rhode Island, Vermont, and Washington state.**

If you know someone in these states who needs health insurance, please let them know that they can go check out their options. Many people also qualify for coverage under Medicaid, which is available year-round.

But millions more need help -- and the Trump administration is standing in the way of them getting the coverage and care that they need.

Across the country right now, people are stepping up to save lives -- by staying home, by working around the clock in hospitals, by keeping people fed, and more. It's time for the federal government to step up and ensure everyone is covered.

**The Trump administration is refusing to take a simple action that would help a whole lot of people get health insurance. And make no mistake: Lives are on the line.**

**[Our government needs to reopen HealthCare.gov and and treat this crisis like the emergency it is. Sign on if you agree: The Trump administration needs to reopen HealthCare.gov and let people sign up for health insurance right now.](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285257ae/45c30650/1f4c49bc2/66e97998/1533021746/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTVEwNVBfU0dOX000QV9FXzIwMjAwNDA0X1YxUzFfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-warrendemocrats-reopen-healthcare-gov?source=EMQ05P_SGN_M4A_E_20200404_V1S1_P&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Stay safe, and thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

| [**ADD YOUR NAME**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285257ae/45c30650/1f4c49bc2/66e97998/1533021746/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTVEwNVBfU0dOX000QV9FXzIwMjAwNDA0X1YxUzFfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-warrendemocrats-reopen-healthcare-gov?source=EMQ05P_SGN_M4A_E_20200404_V1S1_P&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")

But this race matters to us more -- because so much is at stake if Mike Garcia is elected. He will be a blank check, a promised vote for everything the president asks for. Access to health care, reproductive rights, campaign finance reform, and so much more is dependent on this race.

**So much is on the line for people in California's 25th -- and that's why we're giving everything we've got to win this race.**

name: Tina Smith
subject: From the bottom of my heart, thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
|
#
My friend — I know this is an incredibly difficult time for so many families and communities, but your support over the past couple of weeks has truly meant the world to me. **From the bottom of my heart, thank you.** The truth is, we have what it takes to win our race and keep this Senate seat working for the people of our state, but it’ll require building a different kind of campaign. So, I wanted to give you an update on how our team is continuing our work during these challenging times. As you may already know, we are doing our best to meet the needs of our communities by raising _thousands_ of dollars online (with your help!) for organizations on the frontlines of this pandemic. The safety and health of our staffers and supporters is our top priority, so we closed our offices and moved everything online in an effort to practice social distancing while making sure we’re still staying in contact with the people of Minnesota. Every day, we’re finding new and creative ways to continue spreading our message across the state and engaging virtually with voters because this election is so important to the future of our country. Even though we need to run this campaign a little differently, our message remains the same: Now more than ever, we must come together to tackle the critical issues impacting all of us. I know we can fulfill that ideal, so long as I can count on your help along the way. So, I’m asking: **If you are financially able, can you chip in a donation of $10 to support our re-election campaign today? Please, only contribute if you can afford it. I understand this is a difficult time for many of you and your families. clean_body: | My friend — I know this is an incredibly difficult time for so many families and communities, but your support over the past couple of weeks has truly meant the world to me. From the bottom of my heart, thank you. The truth is, we have what it takes to win our race and keep this Senate seat working for the people of our state, but it’ll require building a different kind of campaign. So, I wanted to give you an update on how our team is continuing our work during these challenging times. As you may already know, we are doing our best to meet the needs of our communities by raising thousands of dollars online (with your help!) for organizations on the frontlines of this pandemic. The safety and health of our staffers and supporters is our top priority, so we closed our offices and moved everything online in an effort to practice social distancing while making sure we’re still staying in contact with the people of Minnesota. Every day, we’re finding new and creative ways to continue spreading our message across the state and engaging virtually with voters because this election is so important to the future of our country. Even though we need to run this campaign a little differently, our message remains the same: Now more than ever, we must come together to tackle the critical issues impacting all of us. I know we can fulfill that ideal, so long as I can count on your help along the way. So, I’m asking: If you are financially able, can you chip in a donation of $10 to support our re-election campaign today? Please, only contribute if you can afford it. I understand this is a difficult time for many of you and your families. Thank you in advance. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = The most important thing you can do during this crisis is to take care of yourself and your loved ones. name: CoryBooker.com
subject: What's on your mind?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CORY BOOKER FOR SENATE
body:
It's important to Cory that we get your response. |
#
Friends, So many have been touched by the global health crisis and Cory has been working hard to ensure that Americans everywhere have the information and resources they need to weather this. **[We're launching our April priorities survey today because Cory wants your input before we start the next phase of this fight. Will you take a few moments to lend us your opinion and input on priorities important to you and your family?](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/2020-april-survey?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bookerlive&utm_content=1+-+Wersquore+launching+our+April+priorities&utm_campaign=em200404&source=em200404")** | [TAKE THE SURVEY](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/2020-april-survey?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bookerlive&utm_content=2+-+TAKE+THE+SURVEY&utm_campaign=em200404&source=em200404")
---
The questions should only take a few minutes to fill out, but your answers are critical to helping inform the strategy moving forward to continue the fight to slow the spread of COVID-19, get protective equipment to medical workers, and assist workers and families who are financially impacted by this crisis.
All of us are adjusting to this new normal and working together to figure out exactly what we need to do next to meet the moment. Opening this email and taking this survey means so much to Cory and everyone on our team. We're grateful for all you do to strengthen our movement every day.
**Take a few minutes to weigh in on our April priorities survey today:**
**[https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/2020-april-survey](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/2020-april-survey?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bookerlive&utm_content=3+-+httpsactcorybookercompages2020aprilsurve&utm_campaign=em200404&source=em200404")**
Thank you for your response,
Team Cory

body:

---

---

If you care about winning in Georgia and retaking the Senate majority, please don't let us fall short again, friend. **Click this link to donate now and make sure your gift is matched: **

Thank you,

Team Warnock

| | |

I'll be honest, friend: I need your help.
We're only a few hours away from the first official FEC deadline of our campaign, and I just got some bad news from my team:** If donations don't pick up right away, we're on track to fall short of our match goal. **
Make no mistake: Control of the U.S. Senate is on the line in this race. With my Republican opponent spending millions of her personal fortune to buy this seat and strengthen Mitch McConnell's grip on power, **the _only_ way we'll be able to flip Georgia blue and take back the Senate is if grassroots folks like you pitch in - but time is almost up for you to have your gift matched. **
**Friend, I wouldn't ask if it weren't important: ****[Will you please give $5 or more right now- as much as you can spare - to have your donation go twice as far to make up this shortfall, flip Georgia blue and take back the Senate for Democrats?](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.%3D%3D/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/warnock-for-georgia_email?refcode=jtk126-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&recurring=1")**
This is the biggest test we've faced so far. And if we fail, we'll lose the best chance we've got at ending Mitch McConnell's Senate majority once and for all.
That's why this match offer is so important. To flip Georgia blue and fight back against every last right-wing attack, we need to close this fundraising gap by midnight tonight - and this match is the way we do it.
**I'm counting on you to chip in right now, ****friend: [Please, if you care about taking back the Senate for Democrats, will you give $5 or more - $5 becomes $10 - to help us hit our critical $50,000 match goal and flip Georgia blue?](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.%3D%3D/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/warnock-for-georgia_email?refcode=jtk126-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&recurring=1")**
Thanks for standing alongside me in this fight.

Reverend Warnock
